name: NKI-Rockland Sample (Enhanced)
description: A large community-ascertained lifespan dataset (ages 6-85) combining resting-state and task fMRI, structural and diffusion MRI, and deep psychiatric, cognitive, and behavioral phenotyping, including naturalistic movie-viewing scans. Its breadth supports the study of social and emotional function across development, adulthood, and aging, and the dimensional links between social-emotional traits and brain function.
citation: Nooner KB, Colcombe SJ, Tobe RH, et al. (2012). The NKI-Rockland Sample: A Model for Accelerating the Pace of Discovery Science in Psychiatry. Frontiers in Neuroscience, 6, 152.
